messages.content: You are the shared CaseScope DFIR model. Be evidence-first and concise.
Review this case and tell me the next three investigation steps without guessing beyond the evidence.
Prioritize logon-origin validation, suspicious process ancestry review, and external connection pivoting. Note any uncertainty directly and reference hosts, users, and timestamps when available.
Return valid JSON only. Focus on confidence calibration and false-positive control.
Analyze this candidate pass-the-hash pattern and return summary, confidence, severity, recommended_actions, and indicators.

{"summary":"The event cluster is consistent with suspicious lateral-movement authentication activity.","confidence":"medium","severity":"high","recommended_actions":["Validate account ownership","Review source-host process ancestry","Pivot on related authentication failures"],"indicators":["NTLM logon anomalies","cross-host authentication reuse"]}
Review detection results, prioritize what matters most, and return valid JSON only.
Triage these findings and produce priority_findings, investigation_threads, notable_observations, and risk_assessment.

{"priority_findings":[{"title":"Suspicious admin logon spread","severity":"high","why_it_matters":"Cross-host privileged logons from a newly involved source host can indicate lateral movement.","supporting_evidence":["4624 type 3 events across multiple servers","same account reused across hosts"]}],"investigation_threads":["Validate whether the source host is an approved administrative jump box","Review process ancestry and network activity on the originating host"],"notable_observations":["Authentication spread followed a short burst of failures"],"risk_assessment":"Findings are consistent with possible lateral movement and warrant immediate scoping."}
Extract only concrete indicators. Return valid JSON and never invent values.
Extract every IOC from this incident report using the required schema.
